title: Ipomoea × multifida
intro: Ipomoea × multifida, also known as Man of the Sea or Sea Vine, is a tropical flowering plant belonging to the morning glory family (Convolvulaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea × multifida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and minor infections.
taxonomy: Ipomoea × multifida is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Convolvulaceae. This plant is a hybrid of Ipomoea pes-caprae and Ipomoea indica. It is commonly known as Hawaiian morning glory or multifida morning glory. Ipomoea × multifida is native to the Hawaiian Islands and is often found growing as a vine in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is known for its heart-shaped leaves and vibrant purple flowers that bloom from mid-summer to fall.
uses: Ipomoea × multifida, also known as Hawaiian morning glory or blue morning glory, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Some studies suggest that Ipomoea × multifida may have antioxidant and antiviral properties, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and effects on human health. Further studies are required to determine the safety and efficacy of using Ipomoea × multifida as a medicinal plant.
benefits: Ipomoea × multifida, also known as Hawaiian blue morning glory, is a plant with potential health benefits.
